title: WOS API Case View
description: Public API resource schema for the staff case view returned by `GET /api/v1/cases/{case_id}` (Refactor 3A.2; CBR §4.4-§4.5; ADR-0093 §5). The case view is keyed by the durable case ledger and aggregates every workflow process bound to it plus every provenance event (direct-append and process-emitted) merged in time order. Replay-vs-projection byte-identity is required for the Phase 5 conformance fixture (CBR §4.7): reading via deterministic replay and reading via materialized projection MUST return byte-identical JSON for the same `case_id`. Audience field projection (the optional `audienceProjection` block) is the only allowed asymmetry between replay-read and projection-read; everything else MUST byte-match. The applicant-audience view lives at `GET /api/v1/applicant/cases/{id}` (see `applicant.schema.json#/$defs/ApplicantCaseDetail`); this staff view does NOT serve applicants. Greenfield per pre-release framing: no `eventCount`/`processCount` legacy shape, no instance-keyed identity. Closed taxonomies per ADR-0082 D-12; vendor extensions are NOT permitted on case-view fields except inside `audienceProjection.extensions` (open-with-`x-` per ADR-0082 D-12).

WosResourceUrn.description: Public WOS API resource URN (ADR 0082 D-4). Hoisted to `_common.schema.json`. Case ledger URNs use the `case` entity-type segment; workflow process URNs use the `process` segment.

CaseStatus.description: Closed case-level rollup posture. `active` — at least one bound workflow process is non-terminal (active, suspended, migrating). `closed` — every bound workflow process reached a terminal lifecycle state (completed, terminated, stalled). `genesis` — the case ledger exists via the direct-append surface but no workflow process is bound yet. The taxonomy is closed-no-extension; pre-release framing forbids `x-` vendor extensions here.

CaseViewEvent.description: One event in the merged time-ordered event stream for the case view. Direct-append events (no `processId`) and process-emitted events (with `processId`) share this shape so replay and projection return byte-identical event arrays. `event` is the closed WOS event-type literal (CBR §4 closed enum; F-13 naming convention). `sequence` is monotonic within the case ledger; `eventHash`/`previousHash` are the Trellis-style chain commitments.

event.description: Registered WOS event literal carried by the provenance payload. Authoritative D26 dispatch value; the inner `recordKind` discriminator is intentionally suppressed in the merged case view. Absent only when the underlying provenance row predates the closed-enum migration (legacy rows; not emitted by greenfield write paths).

processId.description: Workflow process URN that emitted this event when present. Absent for direct-append events written through `POST /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/events`. The presence/absence of this field is the structural marker for the emission branch (CBR §4.6 two-branch authorization).

sequence.description: Monotonic sequence number within the case ledger's merged event stream. Direct-append rows partition on `case_ledger_id`; process-emitted rows partition on `process_id` but are still anchored to the case ledger and ordered by `timestamp` then `sequence` in this view.

timestamp.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the row was appended. Primary sort key for the merged stream.

CaseEventAppendRequest.description: Request body for `POST /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/events`, the direct case-ledger append surface. `wos.kernel.case_created` opens a new ledger; registered post-ledger WOS event literals run relationship authorization and append only after an allow decision plus event-contract validation.

event.description: Registered WOS event literal. The reference server persists `wos.kernel.case_created` before ledger creation and persists post-ledger literals only after relationship authorization allows and the direct writer can enforce the event contract.

actorId.description: Actor identifier for the direct append. Greenfield callers SHOULD supply the canonical `ActorRef`; legacy storage adapters may still preserve the opaque actor identifier that signed the append.

idempotencyToken.description: Replay token scoped to `(tenant, token)` for pre-ledger creation and `(case_id, token)` for post-ledger direct append.

data.description: Optional event-contract data. `caseRef` and `caseLedgerId` are supplied from the path for generic direct appends and must match if present. The concrete event contract validates the payload before persistence.

CaseEventAppendReceipt.description: Receipt returned after a direct case-ledger append commits.

caseLedgerId.description: Durable governed case-ledger URN that received the direct append.

eventId.description: TypeID of the provenance row produced by the append.

eventHash.description: Hash-chain digest for the appended event.

sequence.description: Monotonic sequence number assigned within the case ledger's event stream.

AudienceProjection.description: Optional audience-driven field projection block. CBR §4.4 architectural commitment: one case view, two audience-appropriate routes. The staff route at `GET /api/v1/cases/{case_id}` MAY surface fields suppressed on the applicant route at `GET /api/v1/applicant/cases/{id}`; the projection block records the resolved audience tier and any field-level redaction the responding deployment applied. This is the ONLY allowed asymmetry between replay-read and projection-read for the byte-identity acceptance fixture (CBR §4.7) — every other field MUST byte-match across read paths. `additionalProperties` is permitted under the `x-` prefix only (open-with-vendor-extension per ADR-0082 D-12).

audience.description: Closed audience taxonomy for the responding view. `staff` is the default for `GET /api/v1/cases/{case_id}` (caseworker / supervisor / adjudicator). `applicant` is reserved for cross-tier reuse; the applicant route returns a distinct `ApplicantCaseDetail` shape. `support` is the human-in-the-loop assistance tier.

redactedFields.description: Field paths suppressed from `events[].payload` (or other case-view subtrees) by the responding deployment's class-bag policy. Paths are JSON Pointer fragments rooted at the response document. Empty array when no redaction applied.

^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$.description: Vendor extension (open-with-`x-` per ADR-0082 D-12). Deployments may attach audience-tier debug metadata under an `x-` key; consumers MUST tolerate unknown extensions.

CaseView.description: Staff case view returned by `GET /api/v1/cases/{case_id}` per CBR §4.5. Keyed by the durable case ledger; aggregates every bound workflow process URN and the merged provenance event stream. The `processes` array carries process URNs only (full process projections live at `GET /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}`). The `events` array is the merged time-ordered stream across direct-append rows and every bound process's per-process provenance. `lastUpdated` is the timestamp of the most recent event (or the case-ledger genesis timestamp when no events exist beyond genesis). Replay-vs-projection byte-identity is required for the Phase 5 N:1 conformance fixture; `audienceProjection` is the only allowed asymmetry between the two read paths.

caseLedgerId.description: Durable governed case-ledger URN (`urn:wos:<tenant>_case_<ulid>`). The case IS its ledger (CBR §1; ADR-0093 D-1).

processes.description: Workflow process URNs bound to this case ledger, sorted by `created_at` then `process_id`. Empty for a direct-append case with no workflow started yet. Full process projections are NOT inlined here; consumers fetch them via `GET /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}` (D-3 subresource discipline). N:1 (N processes per one case) is supported from day one per CBR §3.

events.description: Merged time-ordered provenance event stream across direct-append rows and every bound process. Direct-append events have no `processId`; process-emitted events carry the emitting process URN. Sort order is primary by `timestamp` ascending, secondary by `sequence` ascending. This array is the load-bearing byte-identity surface for the Phase 5 replay-vs-projection conformance fixture.

status.description: Case-level lifecycle rollup. See `CaseStatus` for the closed taxonomy.

lastUpdated.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp of the most recent event in the merged stream. When `events` is empty (an impossible state under the 404 contract — the case view 404s when there are no processes AND no events), this field is absent.

audienceProjection.description: Audience-projection metadata. Optional; the only allowed asymmetry between replay-read and projection-read for the byte-identity fixture.
